summary: The team held a sprint twelve planning meeting that began with a quick retrospective on sprint eleven, where seven of nine tickets were completed. Key carry-overs include alert deduplication and a pipeline latency investigation that revealed a significant single point of failure in the event ingestion layer. The team agreed on primary sprint twelve commitments: deduplication carry-over, pipeline architecture remediation, and a CI gate improvement, with detection rule versioning pushed to a stretch goal or sprint thirteen. Capacity constraints and QA bandwidth were key factors in scoping decisions.
actionItems: Chris Lee: Share the rough detection rule versioning spec doc with the team after the call
Ananya Sharma: Draft pipeline architecture sub-tasks today and share in the team channel for review before the board is finalized
Chris Lee: Update the sprint board and send a summary to the team channel
Nina Kowalski: Create a chore ticket for CI gating to prevent unvalidated config merges

keyMoments.text: Ananya flags a deeper architectural issue in the event ingestion layer, identifying a single point of failure with no redundancy or circuit breaker.
keyMoments.speaker: Ananya Sharma

keyMoments.text: Nina raises that a regression in alert severity scoring was caught on day nine of the sprint, noting that CI gating should replace manual discipline for config changes.
keyMoments.speaker: Nina Kowalski

keyMoments.text: Chris proposes scoping rule versioning as a stretch goal to set realistic expectations with product, a practical prioritization decision endorsed by the team.
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee

keyMoments.text: Team reaches alignment on sprint twelve commitments: dedup carry-over, pipeline architecture work, and CI gate improvement, with rule versioning deferred.
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee
